content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES

BUSH, ACKLEY, MILICH & HALLINAN
ESTABLISHED 1923

ATTORNEYS AT LAW
366 WEST F STREET

DAVID F. BUSH 1923-1955

P.O. BOX 486

GEORGE H. ACKLEY 1935-1975

OAKDALE, CALIFORNIA 95361

LEO MILICH

TELEPHONE
(209) 847-1756

HARTLEY H. BUSH

THOMAS N. HALLINAN

OF COUNSEL

April 11, 1990


Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

ATTN:  John W. Wallace
       Counsel - Legal Division

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     As City Attorney of the City of Riverbank, I am requesting formal written advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83314(b).  The question involves a proposed general plan amendment, rezone application, prezone approval, and tentative subdivision map located on approximately 84 acres in the City of Riverbank and property contiguous to the City of Riverbank.  The proposed subdivision, located within the general 84-acre area, consists of 19 acres.

     Councilman Carl Lemmons holds shares in a California corporation, Ambergreen, Inc., originally formed 8 to 10 years ago with nine shareholders.  Over the years, various shareholders have sold their interests to the remaining shareholders and, in fact, the corporation has ceased to operate as a corporation and the remaining interested parties have filed income tax returns as partners.  In the year 1989 there were four "partners" and beginning with 1990 there are three, including Councilman Lemmons.

     The Ambergreen corporation purchased a note secured by a deed of trust on one-half acre located within the 84 acres in question.  A residence was located on the property.  The owner of the property, Michael Riben, obtained a second deed of trust from Martin Financial Company, which covered the half-acre encumbered by Ambergreen and other property contiguous to it owned by Riben.  Mr. Riben defaulted on payment of the second deed of trust and the holder of the second deed of trust has been making the payments to the "partners".

Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan
April 11, 1990
Page 2

--------------------------------------


The original note purchased by the Ambergreen group was
in the amount of $30,000. The house located on the property
burned down and sometime in 1987 the insurance proceeds
were paid to the Ambergreen group, which reduced the loan
to a point where the present balance is $7,094. Interest
on the note is at the rate of ten (10%) percent per annum
and the monthly payments are in the amount of $272.05.

The holder of the second deed of trust, Martin Financial,
has foreclosed on the property and has continued to make
the payments on the first to the Ambergreen group. There
is presently litigation between Martin Financial and Riben,
however, the Ambergreen group is not a party to that liti-
gation. Martin Financial is attempting to sell the property
to the applicant for the general plan, rezone, prezone,
and tentative map, Liberty Development Company. Neither
Martin Financial nor Michael Riben applied for any of the
entitlements requested from the Riverbank City Council.

Mr. Lemmons has earned interest income through Ambergreen
for the last 12 months of less than $200.

Question: Whether Councilman Lemmons is precluded from
participating in the proposed general plan amendment, re-
zone application, prezone application, and tentative map,
because of his ownership interest in Ambergreen, Inc.,
the record holder of the note and deed of trust, with a
present outstanding balance of $7,094.

These matters are scheduled before the Riverbank Planning
Commission on April 17, 1990, and before the City Council
two weeks after that date.

I would appreciate hearing from you at your earliest con-
venience. Thank you for your cooperation.

Yours very truly,


THOMAS N. HALLINAN
City Attorney
City of Riverbank

TNH/web

FPPC Neumiller & Beardslee

FOUNDED AS                                                                                                                    MAILING ADDRESS:
ASHLEY & NEUMILLER                      A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION                                    P.O. DRAWER 20
JANUARY 1903                    MAY 24 2 40 PM '90 ATTORNEYS AND COUNSELORS                           STOCKTON, CALIFORNIA 95201-9978
                                                        FIFTH FLOOR WATERFRONT OFFICE TOWER II                TELEPHONE (209) 948-8200
                                                        509 WEST WEBER AVENUE
                                                        STOCKTON, CALIFORNIA 95202


                                                        May 23, 1990


Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention:  John W. Wallace
            Counsel - Legal Division

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     This office represents Monschien Cabinets, an opponent
of a proposed residential development project in the City
of Riverbank.  The City Council of the City of Riverbank
was scheduled to hear the applicant's request for a general
plan amendment and rezoning of the subject property on May
14, 1990.  However, just prior to the hearing, we were
informed that a letter of inquiry had been reported to your
office by City Attorney Thomas Hallinan regarding a
potential conflict of interest of one of the council
members.  As Mr. Hallinan's letter to your office correctly
indicates, councilmember Carl Lemmons is a part-owner in a
partnership which holds a note and deed of trust which is
secured by one of the parcels of property in question.  We
have heard conflicting reports in recent days that
councilmember Lemmons and/or the corporation has recently
divested itself of the note.  However, during the time
period in which councilmember Lemmons' corporation did hold
the note, councilmember Lemmons voted against requiring an
environmental impact report in connection with the proposed
annexation and general plan change on the subject
property.

     We believe that both the previous action taken by
councilmember Lemmons and any further involvement by
councilmember Lemmons in connection with this project would
violate the Fair Political Practices Act.  First, the note
and deed of trust on the subject property was in fact a
source of income for councilmember Lemmons at the time he
voted to overrule the Planning Commission's recommendation
to require an environmental impact report on the project.

Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan
May 23, 1990
Page -2-


Secondly, since the alleged sale of the subject note and
deed of trust is not yet complete, councilmember Lemmons
has a direct financial interest in a piece of property
which is being rezoned in violation of Government Code
Section 87103(c) and FPPC Guidelines Section 18702.1.

While we believe that councilmember Lemmons' previous
action on this property in connection with the
environmental impact report requirement negates the City
Council's action in connection with the EIR, it would serve
little purpose for our office to bring any type of judicial
action to nullify that action. In fact, since no notice of
determination (CEQA Guidelines Section 15075) has been
filed in connection with the EIR requirement, we
could not bring an action until such notice has been
filed. We would, however, encourage your office to
investigate this violation of the FPPC Guidelines. In
addition, we would appreciate your prompt reply to the
request made by various parties with respect to future
actions which councilmember Lemmons may be required to take
in connection with this project. As you may be aware, the
City Council continued its hearing on this matter until
June 9, 1990. This office appeared at the May 14 hearing
in which motion to continue was granted. At that hearing
we contended that councilmember Lemmons could not vote on
the motion to continue (which he raised) since the Fair
Political Practices Act bars the public official from
participating in any way in a project in which he has a
potential conflict of interest. In your reply letter, we
would appreciate your comments on what we perceive to be an
additional violation of the Fair Political Practices Act.

In conclusion, we appreciate your office's work in
investigating this matter. If we can be of any assistance
or if you require further information, we would be more
than happy to assist you in whatever way possible. Thank
you for your time and your attention to this request.

Very truly yours,

THOMAS H. TERPSTRA
Attorney at Law

THT/ch
cc: Thomas N. Hallinan
    Frank Monschien
    Elvan Overholtzer
